summary: Package installs deceptive Windows persistence and exfiltrates host fingerprint via obfuscated loader
details: On `require()` of this package, the declared `main` (launcher.js) reads a sibling obfuscated blob (server.obf.js, an obfuscator.io-packed file with an RC4-decoded string array) and executes it via `eval(fs.readFileSync(serverCode, 'utf8'))`. The launcher invokes PowerShell at load time to write an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run value named `OneDriveUpdate` that runs `wscript.exe OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs`, which silently re-launches `node launcher.js`. The launcher also sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'`. The filename, registry value, and process title deliberately impersonate legitimate Microsoft components — standard malware masquerading tradecraft, not a normal package autostart. The eval'd server collects a Windows host fingerprint (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid + `os.hostname()` + C:\ volume serial), SHA-256 hashes it, and POSTs it together with a license key, nonce, and `app:'forte'` identifier to a remote endpoint whose URL is hidden behind RC4-decoded strings in the obfuscated blob. The package additionally ships a 6.8 MB native module `sens.node` containing strings (`Aimbot`, `Teleport`, `LeftFoot`, `LeftHand`, `Head`, `Vehicle Spawn`) that are wired to a `Start Cheat` button in the included web UI — the package's actual purpose is a game-cheat tool, contradicting the declared description. Installer impact: any machine that loads this package gets a hidden, Microsoft-impersonating autostart and has its hardware identifier sent to an undisclosed third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
